channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Xbox Confirms Gears Of War: E-Day Will Stay Exclusive And Not Come To PlayStation 5
item.content:encoded:
New Xbox CEO Asha Sharma said the thing console loyalists have been begging for: Gears of War: E-Day will remain an exclusive. Despite reported plans to previously bring the upcoming shooter to PlayStation 5, Microsoft has seemingly reversed course in an attempt to win back hardcore fans.

The surprise exclusivity was Sharma’s first announcement of the Xbox summer showcase, arriving after several minutes of gameplay for the upcoming Gears prequel was revealed in an extended trailer. The change-up comes after a Gears of War: E-Day placeholder page on Walmart for a PS5 version appeared online last night before later being deleted.

Xbox CEO Asha Sharma announces Gears E-Day will go back to being an Xbox Series X/S console exclusive pic.twitter.com/Hlji1fZUlW

— AmericanTruckSongs9 (@ethangach) June 7, 2026

The question of exclusivity has been the biggest one facing Sharma since she took over the top job from Phil Spencer earlier this year. While Microsoft appeared to be shifting to being primarily a third-party publisher and bringing all of its major games to other platforms, Sharma’s first 100 days have been marked by the drum beat of “returning to Xbox.” Part of that reset now appears to be taking a retreat from putting everything on PS5.

“I think it’s a tough topic,” Sharma said in an interview last week. “Look, we’re the number-two publisher in the world. And in order to be a great publisher, you must have your games reach large audiences to play.”

But she continued, “At the same time, we’re increasingly becoming a platform. In order to be a platform, you must have exclusive content and services. And so we’re looking at that very closely. I think that we have to be very thoughtful about each title and how we want to think about it and learn from similar cases in the industry.”

That doesn’t mean everything at Xbox will now be exclusive. Forza Horizon 6 is still seemingly coming to PS5 later this year, and Fable has already been announced as a multiplatform release as well. But this latest move does signal that other first-party games can no longer be assumed to be coming to anything beyond Xbox and PC at launch.



https://kotaku.com/xbox-confirms-gears-of-war-e-day-will-stay-exclusive-and-not-come-to-playstation-5-2000703617
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gears Of War E-Day Gameplay Shows Off The Chaotic Moment Locust Arrived
item.content:encoded:
Xbox kicked off its big Xbox Summer Game Fest Showcase with an extended look at gameplay for Gears of War: E-Day, the next mainline game in the cover shooter series. It’s out this October exclusively on Xbox and PC.

In the new gameplay footage, we see Marcus and Dom fighting through city streets and store interiors against the Locust as they first began their attack on the planet. During the fighting, we see Gears staples, like active reloads and retro lancers.

Later, the gameplay shows some very classic Gears of War combat, including Marcus fighting off iconic enemies in his old Gears suit. Or I guess it’s new here as it’s a prequel.

Interestingly, after the trailer, new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma appeared on camera to confirm that Gears of War will be a console exclusive, despite previous leaks indicating a PS5 port. Those seem to be wrong, or perhaps suggest that Xbox has changed course.

Gears of War: E-Day is set to arrive on Xbox Series X/S and PC and nothing else on October 6.



https://kotaku.com/gears-of-war-e-day-gameplay-shows-off-the-chaotic-day-locust-arrived-2000703612
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Watch Xbox’s Biggest Summer Gaming Showcase In Years Right Right Here
item.content:encoded:
All eyes are on Xbox’s June Showcase, which is set to go live in less than an hour. That extra attention is partly because of the expected game reveals and deep dives into upcoming releases on the console platform’s 25th anniversary. But the other reason is to see how Microsoft decides to handle first-party exclusivity moving forward. Will Gears of War: E-Day be available on PlayStation 5 at some point? Or has the tech giant decided to yank that port at the last minute?

The latter is what Giant Bomb host Jeff Grubb claimed happened during last night’s Giant Bomb Couch livestream. Despite Gears being rated for PS5 and a Walmart placeholder listing even appearing online, the rumor now is that new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma may have decided to pivot last-minute to give the console’s most diehard fans a long-sought-after win.

You can watch the full Xbox 2026 summer showcase starting at 1:00 p.m. ET on June 7 below:

Exclusivity drama and first-party games aren’t the only things to look out for at the show. These press conferences also pride themselves on snagging exclusive reveals for big third-party games. One of those reveals that all of the leaks have been pointing to in recent weeks is Persona 6. Xbox has been getting a lot cozier with Sega, and Atlus’ next RPG is expected to appear during today’s showcase.

We’ll no doubt also get a lot of news about what new releases from the second half of this year will be coming to Game Pass, as well as updates on first-party games we haven’t heard from in a while. Those include stuff like State of Decay 3 from Undead Labs and, my personal most anticipated upcoming Xbox game, Clockwork Revolution from inXile Entertainment. The event will also be Sharma’s first time presenting Xbox on the Summer Game Fest circuit, and the first showcase in years that won’t be helmed by her predecessor, Phil Spencer.

Let us know what you’re hoping to see from the livestream and what you end up grading it in the comments below!



https://kotaku.com/watch-xboxs-biggest-summer-gaming-showcase-in-years-right-right-here-2000703604
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
